background: Optimize a bounded two-variable scientific response from observed wet-lab history.
core_experience: Past runs suggest the response improves when x1 is moderately high and x2 is not too high.
Very low x1 tends to underperform even when x2 is favorable.
The most promising region is expected near the best observed point, but small moves may still help.
expert_rules: Prefer candidates that stay close enough to observed high-response settings unless uncertainty is high.
Do not extrapolate aggressively outside the current experimental envelope in wet mode.
measurement_notes: Observed y values are high-fidelity measurements.
Small differences below 0.02 may be within experimental noise.
constraints: Keep both variables inside the declared bounds.
prompt_guidance: Use the core experience as soft prior knowledge, not as a hard constraint.
When evidence and experience disagree, prefer candidates that can reduce uncertainty.
labo_prompt_addendum: For LABO, estimate the low-fidelity target on the same scale as y.
lgbo_prompt_addendum: For LGBO, a compact region around the promising band is acceptable if one exact point is too brittle.
